channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alleged Filipino spy admits being tasked to gather data on West Philippine Sea operations and Balikatan drills
item.description: A Filipino man identified as alias “Danny” has admitted to being involved in an espionage network allegedly working for China. According to a report on GMA’s 24 Oras, Danny said that his contact, who ...

item.title: Outback Station Manager Brendan Cullen’s English Channel Challenge
item.description: Brendan Cullen, a 49-year-old sheep station manager from far western New South Wales, recounts in his book The Desert Swimmer how he set out to conquer the English Channel — one of the most formidable...

item.title: UK energy bills forecast to increase sharply amid high oil and gas prices
item.description: UK households are facing a significant increase in annual energy bills, expected to rise by approximately £332 in July 2026, reaching their highest level since July 2023. The forecast, issued by Cornw...

item.title: Ireland weighs social media age restrictions amid ambitions to lead EU digital regulation
item.description: The Irish government is considering introducing age restrictions on social media for users under 16 as part of its National Digital and AI Strategy 2030. The proposal, which includes a digital wallet–...

item.title: Sindh CTD arrests five BRG members foiling Karachi railway attack plan
item.description: On Friday, Sindh’s Counter-Terrorism Department (CTD) arrested five members of the banned Baloch Republican Guard (BRG) in Karachi, who were allegedly planning attacks on railway tracks. The arrests f...

item.title: Cyclone Narelle hits Queensland and moves towards Northern Territory
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle struck Queensland’s Cape York Peninsula early on Friday, making landfall as a dangerous category four storm with wind gusts exceeding 250 km/h. The system has since been downg...

item.title: Police intervene as protesters attempt to disrupt Ramaphosa's title deed handover
item.description: On March 20, 2026, a protest disrupted a title deed handover ceremony in uMzimkhulu, KwaZulu-Natal, where President Cyril Ramaphosa was handing over property deeds to communities previously dispossess...

item.title: Shandong outlines four focus areas to strengthen economic cooperation with Tanzania
item.description: During an official investment mission to Shandong Province, Tanzania’s Minister of State in the President’s Office (Planning and Investment), Prof Kitila Mkumbo, met with Chinese officials to deepen e...

item.title: Kwala Dry Port Eases Cargo Congestion at Dar es Salaam Port
item.description: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Infrastructure recently visited the Kwala Dry Port to assess the progress of its ongoing project and observe its operations. Minister of Transport, Professor Ma...

item.title: New magnetic fluid technique may help prevent strokes in atrial fibrillation patients
item.description: Researchers have developed a promising new technique that could reduce the risk of stroke in people with atrial fibrillation, a common heart rhythm disorder. The condition causes the heart’s upper cha...

item.title: Transport groups continue strike, hold protest in Mendiola over fare and fuel concerns
item.description: Transport groups and activists marched to Mendiola on the second day of the ongoing transport strike to protest what they call the government’s slow response to the growing problems in the public tran...

item.title: Record March Heat Sweeps U.S. Southwest Amid Expanding Heat Dome
item.description: Preliminary weather readings show that the United States has experienced its hottest March day on record, with extreme heat striking Arizona, southern California, and other parts of the Southwest. Tem...

item.title: Pepsodent Starts Nationwide Campaign to Encourage Brushing Twice Daily in Nigeria
item.description: Pepsodent don start their 2026 World Oral Health Day campaign, wey dem call 'Do The 2', to make sure Nigerians dey maintain correct oral hygiene. The campaign launch happen for Lagos inside Unilever h...

item.title: Guntur District Seeks Women Candidates for Mission Vatsalya Scheme Posts
item.description: The District Women and Child Development Department of Guntur has announced that applications are open for several contractual and outsourced positions under the Mission Vatsalya scheme within the Chi...

item.title: Senator Gatchalian Commits Budget for Education, Health, and Governance Projects on Pag-asa Island
item.description: Senator Sherwin Gatchalian has pledged budget allocations for Pag-asa Island in the 2027 national budget, emphasizing the importance of the island in asserting Philippine sovereignty over the West Phi...

item.title: DepEd projects 3.7 million learners to graduate from basic education amid ongoing learning challenges
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) projects around 3.7 million learners to complete basic education in School Year 2025–2026, marking one of the biggest graduating batches since the implementation of...

item.title: Gladwyn White demands clarity on Safa suspension
item.description: Gladwyn White, a suspended member of the South African Football Association (Safa), revealed that he has not received any formal notification outlining the reasons behind his suspension. White stated ...

item.title: China Approves First Brain Implant for Sale, Paving the Way for Global Industry Leadership
item.description: In a groundbreaking move, China has become the first country to approve a commercially available brain implant, the NEO chip, designed to help individuals with paralysis regain mobility. Developed by ...

item.title: Uttarakhand High Court Restrains Gym Owner Deepak Kumar from Social Media Posts Amid Investigation
item.description: The Uttarakhand High Court on Friday refused to quash the case against Deepak Kumar, the gym owner who opposed a group of Bajrang Dal members for allegedly harassing an elderly Muslim shopkeeper in Pa...

item.title: Artist Creates Miniature Felted Nudibranchs Inspired by Ocean Life
item.description: Russian artist Arina Borevich is captivating audiences with her exquisite hand-felted sculptures of nudibranchs, a type of vibrantly colored sea slug. Through her project, Wool Creature Lab, Borevich ...
